ip_outcome_note: Card name is 'Explorer Bulma' — explicit character name in title. Hover-bike rider framing, yellow color, Special (Model Kit) trait all consistent with Bulma's established vehicle-and-tech register. Card name is direct evidence per anti-conflation rule criterion (a).

trivia_bullets.text: Explorer Bulma (BT4-093, Common, Yellow) costs 3 yellow energy for 10,000 power and carries an [Auto] on play: mill up to 2 cards to the Drop Area, switch up to 2 energy to Active Mode, draw 1 card. The dual energy-refresh is notably stronger than Multimech Bulma's single-energy refresh for one additional cost, making it a wider refuel effect at common rarity. [WebSearch: DBZExchange BT4-093; cartajouer.com listing]

BT4-093 carries the Special (Model Kit) trait — a niche trait shared by limited characters in BT4 — distinguishing Bulma as part of the game's Special-era grouping rather than fitting cleanly into a saga-arc alignment. This trait signals the design team treating Bulma as a cross-era support character rather than pinning her to the Tournament of Power or Saiyan Saga structures that dominate BT4. [WebSearch: totalcards.net BT4-093 listing]

Bulma's mobility apparatus — bikes, planes, capsule vehicles — is one of the franchise's oldest visual throughlines. Her Dragon Ball debut panel showed her next to a Capsule Corp motorbike. Explorer Bulma's hover-bike composition continues that motif into the Super era, framing her independence as inseparable from her access to Capsule Corp technology. [Wikipedia: Bulma via WebSearch]

Explorer Bulma received a tournament promo stamp variant (BT4-093_PR) distributed at Origins 2019, indicating it was selected for organized-play promotion — a distinction that signals above-average play utility or community recognition for the card at the time. [WebSearch: plentyofgames.com.au Origins 2019 promo listing]
